element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Secondary objective completion (up to seven 10,000 point bonuses)
Episode-concluding challenge with no exit alternative
Future-based level design and progression climax

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multi-directional scrolling with alternating combat and platforming challenges; tension builds toward episode-end boss encounters with no alternative exit paths

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Destructible boxes and obstacles signal explorable areas; destroyable cameras indicate hidden bonus opportunities
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Breakable environmental objects frequently conceal or signal optional collectibles and bonus point opportunities

difficulty_curve.early_game: Episode 1 introduces basic platforming, jumping, and combat with forgiving enemy placement; emphasis on learning mechanics through safe contexts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Episode 2 (moonbase) increases enemy density and introduces more complex level layouts; weapon variety and power-up scarcity heightens challenge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Episode 3 (time machine/future) features most aggressive enemy placement and complex navigation; culminates in Dr. Proton final boss confrontation
notable_spikes: Episode 1, Level 10 (first Dr. Proton boss fight)
Episode 2, Level 10 (second Dr. Proton encounter, moonbase climax)
Episode 3, Level 10 (final Dr. Proton fight after time-travel pursuit)

notable_level_patterns.description: Final level of each episode (Levels 10, 20, 30) features no exit; instead requires locating and defeating Dr. Proton. No alternative completion path available.
Standard levels (except episode finales) embed optional objectives such as destroying all cameras; completion rewards up to 70,000 bonus points, incentivizing exploration and thoroughness
Each episode occupies distinct setting (Los Angeles ruins → Moonbase → Future) with corresponding visual and enemy design shifts
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Episode 1 vs Episode 2 vs Episode 3 spatial contexts
